title: PokéAPI Resource Identity, URL Construction, Pagination, and Fair Use · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds fixed-origin PokéAPI resource and fair-use policy from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of fixed-origin PokéAPI resource and fair-use policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Keep scheme and authority fixed to the approved PokéAPI origin and map commands only to enumerated resource paths.
Distinguish resource type, numeric ID, canonical lowercase name, list query, and response self URL without unsafe equivalence.
Construct path segments and limit or offset queries with net/url while rejecting slash, control, and oversized input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Fixed Origin P…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 2055 during a clean cross-platform package smoke. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit fixed-origin resource URLs, bounded pagination, and fair-use request policy; the release claim must identify the exact source and artifact revision; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep scheme and authority fixed to the approved PokéAPI origin and map commands only to enumerated resource paths.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Pokedex Go Fair Use Po…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-fair-use-policy-1v18wj" that uses case 2406 to admit fixed-origin resource URLs, bounded pagination, and fair-use request policy. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 1423 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. stimulus.title: Pokedex Go Fair Use Policy failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: No published rate limit means unlimited parallel requests are acceptable.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cache every requested resource, limit frequency and concurrency, identify the client responsibly, and avoid unsupported availability claims.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Pokedex Go Fair Use Policy happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Pokedex Go Fair Use Policy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
